title: crataegus oxyacantha var. oliveriana
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha var. oliveriana, also known as the hawthorn tree or English hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the rose family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Crataegus oxyacantha var. oliveriana lie in its berries, flowers, and leaves, which have been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health and lower blood pressure due to their high levels of flavonoids, particularly oligomeric proanthocyanidin complexes (OPCs). The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and protecting against cardiovascular disease, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Crataegus oxyacantha var. oliveriana include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to promote overall cardiovascular health and well-being.
